checks.finding: design.md Architecture defines registry/schema, paired validators, provenance validation, and caller boundaries; Components supplies JSON, JSON Schema, Bash, PowerShell, and GitHub Actions technologies, while Frontend Plan explicitly marks frontend inapplicable.

design.md Architecture states dependencies flow from callers to adapters to registry/schema and review-contract validation, and explicitly prohibits validator callbacks to callers; no cycle is present.

design.md Data Plan specifies new registry and retrospective entities, existing artifacts read without rewriting, the commit-bounded migration strategy, and explicitly states no database or application-data migration.

design.md API / Contract Plan explicitly states no network API, RPC, or event changes and defines the internal versioned registry contract; Backend Plan specifies the paired CLI command, flags, diagnostics, and exit semantics.

design.md Security Boundaries identifies file-to-validator, reports-to-provenance, and validator-to-gate boundaries with authorization controls, repository/build-data classifications, and relevant traversal, injection, deserialization, forgery, and fail-open concerns.

design.md Test Strategy names fixture-level validator coverage, paired-runtime integration and parity boundaries, AC-001 through AC-014 verification, regression commands, and retained CI/implementation/quality-gate evidence; isolated fixtures supply controlled artifact dependencies.

All components referenced by Architecture, Frontend Plan, Backend Plan, and API / Contract Plan are defined in Components, Integration, or established by investigation.md INV-002 through INV-004 and INV-007.
design.md Architecture Decision Records references docs/adr/0002-repository-workflow-state-integrity.md, and that document exists at the canonical ADR path.
